titel: Follow-up on the crime prevention strategy "Barriers Against Crime."
html: One of the government's top priorities is to combat and prevent crime and increase safety in our country. Developing society's crime prevention measures and adapting them to today's challenges is a central part of this work. In March 2024, the government presented the report "Barriers Against Crime - A Social Prevention Strategy Against Criminal Networks and Other Crime" (report 2023/24:68). The purpose of this strategy is to deepen and develop social crime prevention efforts and to promote more actors implementing evidence-based measures to prevent crime. The strategy has a particular focus on breaking the recruitment and socialization into criminal networks, preventing children and young people from becoming involved in crime, and helping individuals who commit crimes to leave that life behind. This follow-up report outlines the measures that the government has taken in accordance with the strategy. The government's overall assessment is that the large number of measures implemented and initiated have contributed to developing and improving social crime prevention efforts. In particular, the conditions for work to break the recruitment into criminal networks have been improved.
tldr: The government has conducted a follow-up on its plan to combat crime. They believe that the new methods are helping to reduce recruitment into criminal gangs.
lattlast:
The government has created a plan called "Barriers Against Crime." The plan aims to prevent crime before it happens and to make society safer. The goal is to disrupt criminal networks and ensure that fewer young people become involved in crime.

The plan utilizes methods based on knowledge. This means using proven research to determine what strategies are actually effective. It also aims to help individuals who have already committed crimes to stop and leave the criminal environment.

The government has now reviewed the progress of the plan. In its report, it states that the many initiatives and projects undertaken have improved and enhanced the work. It believes that it is now easier to prevent young people from being drawn into criminal groups.
